title: Planchonella schlechteri
intro: Planchonella schlechteri, also known as the Fijian plum, is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to the Pacific Islands, including Fiji and Samoa. The Fijian plum has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its numerous health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Planchonella schlechteri include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with the Fijian plum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its health benefits and traditional uses in modern herbal medicine.
